items.prompt: interprets definite integrals, accumulation functions, and net-change relationships across tables, graphs, formulas, and bounded rate contexts
items.expected_evidence: interpret definite integrals consistently as accumulation or net change across tables, graphs, formulas, and verbal descriptions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner treats integral evidence as coherent accumulation reasoning rather than as disconnected area or antiderivative procedures.

items.prompt: explains how rate, accumulation, antiderivative reasoning, and the Fundamental Theorem connect in introductory integral-calculus contexts
items.expected_evidence: explain why accumulation functions and antiderivatives are connected through changing rate
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains integral meaning as one coherent accumulation-and-change system across multiple representations.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to signed_area_orientation_confusion.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish signed area, total amount, and accumulated change when the context requires different interpretations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where an area or accumulation claim lost the sign or contextual meaning of the integral.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar integral-calculus context and identifies which accumulation evidence governs the claim
items.expected_evidence: justify integral-calculus claims in bounded modeling or analysis tasks using the correct accumulation evidence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent integral reasoning in a varied context and names the accumulation evidence that actually controls the conclusion.
